hypothesis: Replacing Jina Reader with Firecrawl for post-rank web context improves Skills brief quality (shadow rubric: specificity, accuracy, timely hooks) while keeping HN enrichment and 7-day momentum ranking unchanged.
change_summary: Keep DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB=1 on Skills; swap web fetch provider from Jina Reader (default) to Firecrawl via DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB_PROVIDER=firecrawl (requires FIRECRAWL_API_KEY).
notes: Catalog pick: firecrawl/firecrawl (Lab tool inventory — context-enrichment / multi-source-search). Unblocked — EXP-20260628-web-enrich-skills complete (2026-07-18, enrich kept on). Baseline starts 2026-07-27. Runs in parallel with EXP-20260701-landing-layout (site-only; different surface). Soft-dedup (EXP-20260715) waits until this completes. Backlog: ENRICH-2. Implementation not shipped yet — add Firecrawl adapter behind DIGEST_ENRICH_WEB_PROVIDER before treatment. Baseline = current Jina Reader web fetch + HN. Treatment = Firecrawl web fetch + HN. METRIC PRIORITY (low traffic): (1) PRIMARY — shadow rubric scores (control Jina vs treatment Firecrawl) and qualitative brief quality; structural check that enrichment still attaches. (2) SECONDARY — PostHog outbound_repo_click / pageviews; collect but do not gate. Pre-treatment: local shadow run with provider=firecrawl and DIGEST_ENRICH_SHADOW=1; score with briefings/lab/shadow-rubric.md. Secrets: FIRECRAWL_API_KEY in GHA. Import snapshots via npm run experiment -- snapshot after each window.
